author: @annaalexander9570
text:
When a narcissist is confronted about their behavior or feels attacked they lash out by using gaslighting, rage, blame-shifting and projection. Narcissists view any accountability as a threat to their fragile self-image resulting in the need to control the narrative. That's what we see in how Candace is responding with such vindictiveness to Jeremy Boreing. 

Jeremy is right in saying Candace can't be stopped because someone else can never stop a narcissist. The only way a narcissist stops is if they don't keep receiving the needed attention that fuels their narcissistic supply. If that happens they'll implode into a narcissistic rage and they can become extremely dangerous. If the attention they need continues to be withheld eventually they'll get bored and move on to someone/something else to get their narcissistic supply from(if they haven't committed any violent crimes during their rage.) Narcissists and psychopaths share many overlapping traits with one being that neither has a conscience. 

Narcissists such as Candace are typically very persuasive, effective and charismatic speakers so it's highly unlikely that she'll ever lose her audience and when a narcissist's behavior is rewarded, in this case by views, clicks and money, they only become more vengeful, rageful and sadistic. So whether Candace starts becoming irrelevant or she continues having a successful and popular show, she'll stay the same vile creature and do the same vile things.

 Standing up to a malignant narcissist like Candace is extremely hard. People may not like Jeremy Boreing, but he's incredibly brave to stand up to her and call her behavior out. Most people don't have the courage to do that. Even most of Charlie's supposed friends refused to confront Candace after she started attacking TPUSA and Erika because they were too scared of the consequences.
